title: Failure of Reconstruction
description: Test your skills on the failures of Reconstruction to impact lasting change for African Americans!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Read the excerpt and answer the following question.**

“Poverty afflicted white Southerners as well as black, and tenancy, and the increasingly oppressive [[☃ definition 1]] system, were regional rather than racial institutions. But blacks, confronting a unique combination of legal and extralegal coercions, were more vulnerable to fraud and found it more difficult to obtain alternative employment. . . . Blacks in the cotton South owned a smaller percentage of the land in 1900 than they had at the end of Reconstruction and possessed few options other than moving from plantation to plantation each January in search of improved conditions.” 

>-Source: Eric Foner, historian, *Reconstruction: America's Unfinished Revolution, 1863-1877,* 2011

**The excerpt best illustrates which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: a right to keep possession of property belonging to a person until a debt owed by that person is paid

choices.content: the creation of an exploitative system of tenant farming
the suppression of African American political rights
the efforts at rebuilding southern infrastructure after the war
the growing pressure to improve racial conditions in the South

assessment_items.question_content:
**Read the excerpt and answer the following question.**

“Poverty afflicted white Southerners as well as black, and tenancy, and the increasingly oppressive [[☃ definition 1]] system, were regional rather than racial institutions. But blacks, confronting a unique combination of legal and extralegal coercions, were more vulnerable to fraud and found it more difficult to obtain alternative employment. . . . Blacks in the cotton South owned a smaller percentage of the land in 1900 than they had at the end of Reconstruction and possessed few options other than moving from plantation to plantation each January in search of improved conditions.” 

>-Source: Eric Foner, historian, *Reconstruction: America's Unfinished Revolution, 1863-1877,* 2011

**The pattern described in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following long-term developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: a right to keep possession of property belonging to a person until a debt owed by that person is paid

choices.content: the growing economic gap between rich landowners and poor white and black farmers in the South
the migration of German and Irish immigrants to the West to find cheap and available land
the increase in troops to address the racial discrimination in these districts
the formation of cooperative organizations calling for increased railroad regulations

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Should the North ever grant free play, or a separation occur, I should look for the whites to go as far as they dare in restricting colored liberty by black codes or detached laws, without establishing personal servitude. . . . we may predict laws prescribing the manner in which they shall hire themselves (perhaps requiring strict contracts, holding them to labor for stated periods at stated wages), severely punishing idleness *and making the violations of contract by a negro a penal offense.*”

>-Source: A South Carolinian (Belton O’Neall Townsend), “The Political Condition of South Carolina,” The Atlantic Monthly, 1877

**The excerpt is best understood as a response to which of the following historical developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the end of the Reconstruction
the financial Panic of 1873
the ratification of the Fourteenth Amendment
the Supreme Court decision in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Now, the effect of this upon the labor of the South is this: A great many laborers work by the month, but all of them are under contract. If I live three miles from a store, and I must work from sunup to sundown, I cannot go where I can do my trading to the best advantage. A man is prevented, no matter whether his family is sick from sundown to sunrise, from going and selling anything that he has, as the landlord will not give them time between sunrise and sundown. . . . If a man commits a crime he out to be punished, but every man ought to have a right to dispose of his own property. . . . I may raise as much cotton as I please in the seed, but I am prohibited by law from selling it to anybody but the landlord.” 

>-Source: Congressman James T. Rapier, Testimony before the US Senate regarding Agricultural Labor Force in the South, 1880

**The excerpt best serves as evidence for which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of a sharecropping system that limited African American access to southern land
the establishment of the Freedmen’s Bureau to aid formerly enslaved people in their transition to public life
the formation of a special committee in Congress to monitor the laws in southern states to identify potential discrimination
the ratification of an amendment that extends citizenship to all people born in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Now, the effect of this upon the labor of the South is this: A great many laborers work by the month, but all of them are under contract. If I live three miles from a store, and I must work from sunup to sundown, I cannot go where I can do my trading to the best advantage. A man is prevented, no matter whether his family is sick from sundown to sunrise, from going and selling anything that he has, as the landlord will not give them time between sunrise and sundown. . . . If a man commits a crime he out to be punished, but every man ought to have a right to dispose of his own property. . . . I may raise as much cotton as I please in the seed, but I am prohibited by law from selling it to anybody but the landlord.” 

>-Source: Congressman James T. Rapier, Testimony before the US Senate regarding Agricultural Labor Force in the South, 1880

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how the sharecropping system kept African Americans from reaching economic self-sufficiency
how the lack of intervention from Congress allowed local governments to restrict African American political rights
how the Panic of 1873 affected the agricultural economy and production in the South
how the United States’ foreign policy of isolationism hurt farmers in the southern states

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Should the North ever grant free play, or a separation occur, I should look for the whites to go as far as they dare in restricting colored liberty by black codes or detached laws, without establishing personal servitude. . . . it is easy to foresee that whites would be lightly punished (if juries would ever convict them) for crimes against blacks, while the criminal law would be severe on black offenders, and conviction easy.”

>-Source: A South Carolinian (Belton O’Neall Townsend), “The Political Condition of South Carolina,” The Atlantic Monthly, 1877

**After 1877, which of the following most fulfilled the goal of “restricting colored liberty” that the excerpt refers to?** 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the introduction of Jim Crow laws
the establishment of the Ku Klux Klan
the Compromise of 1877
the ratification of the Thirteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “The Court’s decision in the *Civil Rights Cases* (1883), however, not only fully validated the blatant ploys to disenfranchise black Americans and relegate them to some shadow existence between freedom and slavery, but also threw open the door to a more codified approach to removing African-Americans first from the voting rolls and then from mainstream civil life.”

>-Source: Lawrence Goldstone, historian, *Inherently Unequal,* 2011

**The developments described in the excerpt best illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the loss of African American rights after Reconstruction
the discrimination that African immigrants faced in the South
the rise of an African American Civil Rights Movement in the United States
the introduction of laws that protected African Americans from discrimination

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “The Court’s decision in the *Civil Rights Cases* (1883), however, not only fully validated the blatant ploys to disenfranchise black Americans and relegate them to some shadow existence between freedom and slavery, but also threw open the door to a more codified approach to removing African-Americans first from the voting rolls and then from mainstream civil life.”

>-Source: Lawrence Goldstone, historian, *Inherently Unequal,* 2011

**The patterns described in the excerpt most directly foreshadowed which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the legalization of segregation across the United States
the growing gap between rich and poor in the South
the occupation of the South by the United States Army
the Compromise of 1877

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1717ae5942b562358a206c71c551f65d.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Nast, “The Union As It Was,” [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Union_as_It_Was.jpg) 1874

**The ideas expressed through the image reveal that in 1874, which of the following was most true of the South? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Violence by white supremacist groups had effectively stripped away all African American rights gained after the Civil War.
Efforts to extend civil and political rights to African Americans had been largely successful across the United States.
Racial discrimination increased after the United States army ceased occupying the southern states.
African Americans migrated North to escape widespread violence in the southern states.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1717ae5942b562358a206c71c551f65d.jpg)

>-Source: Thomas Nast, “The Union As It Was,” [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Union_as_It_Was.jpg) 1874

**The developments referenced by the image most directly contributed to:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the introduction of Jim Crow laws by state governments.
the ratification of the Fifteenth Amendment.
the creation of the Freedmen’s Bureau.
the Compromise of 1877.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b6cdb7c1b7beb384f73e4c8d5ae96b8.jpg)

>-Source: A.B. Frost, “Of Course He Wants to Vote the Democratic Ticket,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b6/%27Of_Course_He_Wants_To_Vote_The_Democratic_Ticket%27_%28October_1876%29%2C_Harper%27s_Weekly.jpg), 1876

**The image most closely reflects which of the following developments in the political climate in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the end of Reconstruction in the South
the acquisition of territory from Mexico  in the West
the passage of the several civil rights acts by Congress
the Supreme Court decision in *Dred Scott v. Sandford*

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b6cdb7c1b7beb384f73e4c8d5ae96b8.jpg)

>-Source: A.B. Frost, “Of Course He Wants to Vote the Democratic Ticket,” [Wikimedia Commons](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b6/%27Of_Course_He_Wants_To_Vote_The_Democratic_Ticket%27_%28October_1876%29%2C_Harper%27s_Weekly.jpg), 1876

**Which of the following statements best describes the perspective expressed in the image about politics in the South?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Local political tactics limited African American access to the polls.
African Americans heavily supported the Democratic Party.
Reconstruction gave African Americans civil and political rights.
Sharecropping in the South replicated enslavement for African Americans.
